items.text: Has your fever been low-grade and coming and going, or high with shaking chills?
choices: Subjective coldness or measured hypothermia instead of fever

items.text: Have you had a cough?
Do you have any sharp chest pain that's worse when you breathe in?
Do you have any burning with urination, going more often than usual, or pain in your side?
Do you have belly pain, or has your skin or eyes turned yellow along with chills?
choices: Jaundice with right upper quadrant pain and rigors

items.text: Do you have any redness on your skin, and does it hurt a lot more than it looks like it should?
choices: No skin redness or disproportionate pain
Rapidly spreading pain out of proportion to exam, with blistering or dark discoloration

items.text: Do you have a new swollen, painful joint that's hard to move?
choices: New joint swelling or pain with restricted motion

items.text: Do you have a stiff neck, or has any light been bothering your eyes?
Are you on chemotherapy, a transplant medication, a biologic, or long-term steroids?
choices: Transplant history, chemotherapy, or known neutropenia

items.text: Has anyone around you been sick with similar symptoms?
Have you traveled anywhere recently, especially outside the country?
choices: No recent travel to an endemic area
Recent travel to an area with endemic infectious disease

items.text: Have you been bitten by a tick or handled any animals recently?
Did you start a new antibiotic, seizure medication, or gout medication before the fever began?
Did you start a new antidepressant or antipsychotic medication before the fever began?
choices: No new serotonergic agent or antipsychotic

items.text: Do you have an overactive thyroid, and have you been taking your thyroid medication as prescribed?
choices: Known hyperthyroidism with missed thionamide doses

items.text: Have you had a recent dental or vascular procedure, do you have a known heart valve problem, or do you inject drugs?
Do you have a catheter, port, or other implanted device, and is there any pain, redness, or drainage where it is?
choices: Indwelling device with local pain, redness, or drainage

items.text: Have you been in the hospital or had surgery in the past month?
choices: Recent hospitalization or surgery within the preceding month

items.text: Assess general appearance and hemodynamic stability.
Inspect the skin for localized infection versus necrotizing changes.
choices: Localized erythema and warmth consistent with cellulitis
Pain out of proportion with bullae, crepitus, or skin necrosis

items.text: Inspect any wound, line, or device site.
choices: Normal, no device or wound present
Purulent drainage or erythema at a wound or device site

items.text: Auscultate the lungs for focal or diffuse findings.
Palpate the abdomen for focal tenderness or peritoneal signs.
choices: Diffuse tenderness with guarding or rebound

items.text: Assess for costovertebral-angle tenderness.
Examine any symptomatic joint for effusion, warmth, and range of motion.
choices: Normal range of motion, no effusion
Warm, swollen joint with restricted motion
Overlying erythema with inability to bear weight or move the joint

items.text: Assess for nuchal rigidity and mental status change.
choices: No nuchal rigidity, normal mental status
Altered mental status with a focal neurologic deficit

items.text: Auscultate for a new heart murmur.
Inspect the extremities and nail beds for embolic or immunologic stigmata of endocarditis.
Inspect the oropharynx, teeth, and neck for a focal source.
